---
title: "Capturing Documents"
---

---
title: "Capturing Documents"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Capture/jwc1646248500993"
section: "Hyland Capture"
---

You can capture image or PDF documents into your Hyland Capture system by scanning or sweeping them as a batch. You can use either the panes or drop-down lists in the document capture layout to select the pre-configured capture setup options available to you. When you select a specific setup option in either its pane or drop-down list, this same option is automatically selected in the other location.

For more information on the specific setup options available in your environment (such as Content Queues or document classes), contact your system administrator.

Before capturing documents, also note the following:

-
It is considered a best practice to capture image documents at a resolution of 300 dpi or lower to keep file sizes manageable. Each captured page has a 10 MB maximum size limit. Where possible, oversized files are converted to JPEGs to reduce their size and allow their images to be displayed.

-
You can sweep single-page image, multi-page TIFF, and PDF documents. However, all other file types, as well as PDF XFA documents and password-protected PDFs or TIFFs, are not supported.

-
Individual documents, including PDFs and multi-page TIFFs, cannot exceed 999 pages.

-
When swept, PDFs and multi-page TIFFs are converted into single-page PNGs for further processing in the Hyland Capture application.

To capture documents:

1.  Open the Hyland Capture application and log in (see Opening the Hyland Capture Application). The Hyland Capture Scan and Import interface is displayed, showing one of the following layouts:
-
If there are no pending batches, the document capture layout is displayed.

-
If there is a pending batch, the document review layout is displayed.

Note:
If you are already logged in to the Hyland Capture application and using the Verify Data interface, you must switch to the Scan and Import interface (see Switching the Interface).

2.  If you have a pending batch in the document review layout, submit or delete the batch (see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export). The document capture layout is displayed.
3.  Select a Content Queue to group the batch documents to be captured under a common business function (for example, an HR Documents Content Queue to group W2 forms, cover letters, and performance reviews).
To select a Content Queue, do one of the following:

-
In the Content Queue pane, click the appropriate Content Queue in the list of available queues.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Tip:
To filter the list of available Content Queues, enter in the search field a string of characters included in the name of the Content Queue you want to select.

-
From the Content Queue drop-down list, select the appropriate Content Queue.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
4.  To automatically apply the same document class to each document you scan or sweep into the batch, select a document class. This establishes a default classification for the batch, but you can change the classification of individual documents when you review them later in the capture process.
When working with document classes, also note the following:

-
If only one document class has been assigned to the selected Content Queue, that document class is selected by default. If multiple document classes have been assigned to the selected Content Queue, no document class is selected by default.

-
After selecting a document class, you can revert back to no selection by selecting Unclassified.

To select a document class, do one of the following:

-
In the Default Classification pane, select the appropriate document class from the drop-down list.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
-
From the classification drop-down list at the bottom of the document capture layout, select the appropriate document class.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
The document class reverts to the default selection each time you change your Content Queue selection.

5.  Select a scanning device or sweep directory from which to import documents by doing one of the following:
-
In the Input Source pane, click the appropriate input source in the list of available input sources.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)  Tip:
To filter the list of available input sources, enter in the search field a string of characters included in the name of the input source you want to select.

-
From the Input Source drop-down list, select the appropriate input source.

![](data:image/gif;base64,)
Note:
You can manage the input sources available for selection by adding, editing, or deleting them. See Working with Input Sources for more information.

6.  Click Start Capture. The document review layout is displayed as the documents are captured into the system.
While capturing documents, also note the following:

-
Documents cannot be captured into the system if there is an issue with the selected input source (for example, the selected sweep directory is empty or the selected scanner is disconnected). If an error message is displayed, you can either cancel the process or resolve the issue (for example, by adding image or PDF files to the sweep directory or reconnecting the scanner) and click Resume or Retry to resume or retry the process.

-
If a document cannot be uploaded during a batch capture process (due to a network interruption, for example), an error message is displayed. You can either click Discard Batch to discard any previously uploaded documents and cancel the process, or you can click Retry Action to retry the process.

-
If a page cannot be uploaded during a batch capture process (due to firewall restrictions, network issues, or file size limits, for example), an error message is displayed and the erroring file is copied to an error folder in your sweep directory.

To resolve the issue, do any of the following:

- Click Copy error folder path to copy the error folder's location to the clipboard for simplified navigation.
- Click Details to expand or collapse details describing the page upload failure. With the details expanded, you can either click the Copy error details icon to copy the details to the clipboard or click the Save error details icon to save the details to a text file.
- To discard any previously uploaded pages and cancel the process, click Discard Batch.
- To skip the current page and continue the process, click Continue Capture. Any pages in a failed state are displayed in the document review layout with a warning icon overlay. These failed pages are not saved when the batch is submitted.
-
If you attempt to sweep any unsupported or oversized files into the system, these files are moved to an error folder in your sweep directory while the supported files are successfully captured. Once the process is complete, an error message is displayed.

To resolve the issue, do any of the following:

- Click Copy error folder path to copy the error folder's location to the clipboard for simplified navigation.
- Click Details to expand or collapse details describing which files could not be processed and why. With the details expanded, you can either click the Copy error details icon to copy the details to the clipboard or click the Save error details icon to save the details to a text file.
- To discard any previously uploaded pages or documents and cancel the process, click Discard Batch.
- If any files in the batch have been successfully swept into the system, you can click Continue Capture to return to the document review layout and review the batch.
For information on preparing the documents for verification or export, see Preparing a Batch for Verification or Export.
